sections.paragraph:
ἅλς, ἁλός, ὁ (Hom. et al.; Lev 2:13; TestSol 18:34; Philo, Spec. Leg. 1, 289 al.; Jos., Ant. 3, 227; s. ἅλας; DELG ἅλς) salt πᾶσα θυσία ἁλὶ ἁλισθήσεται Mk 9:49 v.l. (s. ἁλίζω). στήλη ἁλός a pillar of salt 1 Cl 11:2 (Gen 19:26). On Mk 9:50b, s. ἅλας.—DELG.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 48
